assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: Response clearly identifies this as a webhook compatibility issue and states 'This is a common issue when upgrading Kubernetes versions due to API changes or webhook compatibility issues'

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: Provides both commands: 'kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations' and 'kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations'

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: Provides two immediate fix options: Option 1 to bypass/disable webhooks temporarily and Option 2 to delete webhook configurations

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: Includes kubectl delete commands for both validating and mutating webhook configurations under 'Option 2: Delete Webhook Configurations'

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: Clearly warns with 'CAUTION: This completely removes webhook validation' and labels it as 'Nuclear Option'

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.30
assertions.reason: Includes command to check current cert-manager version and discusses upgrading to '1.30-compatible version'

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: Under 'Permanent Fix' section, provides helm upgrade command to upgrade cert-manager to v1.15.0 as '1.30-compatible version'

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: Option 1 shows kubectl patch commands that set 'failurePolicy':'Ignore' as an alternative to deletion

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
assertions.reason: Provides verification commands under 'Verify Webhook Health' including checking pods, webhook endpoint, and testing connectivity
